content:
💡 maigret | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Gathering open-source intelligence (OSINT) on individuals by collecting information from various online platforms using only a username.
✨ Key Features: • Supports 3,000+ sites with filtering by tags (categories, countries) • Embeddable in Python projects for programmatic use • Extracts all available information from profile pages and site APIs • Performs recursive search using discovered usernames and IDs
📖 Summary: Maigret is a Python-based tool designed to collect extensive information about a person solely by their username. It scans over 3,000 websites, extracting available data from profiles and APIs, and can perform recursive searches for a comprehensive digital dossier.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 gpt_image_2_skill | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a collection of effective prompts and an integrated toolset (CLI and agent skill) for generating and editing images using OpenAI's GPT Image 2 model.
✨ Key Features: • GPT Image 2 prompt gallery and library • Agentic skill for AI agents (e.g., Claude Code, Codex) • CLI for image generation and editing • Reusable prompts for various use cases (research figures, UI mockups, etc.) • Focus on curated examples for quality and demonstration
📖 Summary: This repository provides a curated gallery of prompts for OpenAI's GPT Image 2, serving as a prompt library and an agentic skill. It also includes a Command Line Interface (CLI) for image generation and editing, designed for use with skill-capable agent runtimes.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 girl-agent | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a conversational AI companion that exhibits complex, human-like behaviors and states, offering a more realistic and engaging interaction than traditional chatbots.
✨ Key Features: • Human-like behavior simulation (sleep, mood, schedule, memory, relationships, conflicts) • Anti-AI prompt to remove ChatGPT-like responses
📖 Summary: Girl-Agent is a TypeScript-based AI character designed to simulate human-like conversational behavior, including sleep, mood, scheduling, memory, and relationship dynamics. It aims to avoid typical AI chatbot patterns by implementing an anti-AI prompt and operating as a character with a distinct state, rather than a simple response generator.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 cc-switch | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a unified and user-friendly desktop interface for managing and interacting with multiple AI coding assistants, simplifying developer workflows.
✨ Key Features: • Cross-platform desktop application (Windows, macOS, Linux) • All-in-One manager for multiple AI coding assistants • Supports Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, OpenClaw, Hermes Agent
📖 Summary: CC Switch is a cross-platform desktop application designed to manage and integrate various AI coding assistants like Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, OpenClaw, and Hermes Agent. It serves as an all-in-one interface for interacting with these different AI models, simplifying provider management and offering WSL support.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 learn-claude-code | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To educate developers on building the infrastructure (harness) around AI models to create functional AI agents.
✨ Key Features: • Teaches the concept of agent harnesses • Explains the origin of agency in AI models • Provides a framework for building agent products • Educational resource for AI agent development
📖 Summary: This repository provides a foundational "harness" for building AI agents, emphasizing that true agency stems from the underlying AI model rather than code orchestration. It serves as an educational tool to teach developers how to construct the necessary infrastructure around an AI model to create a functional agent product.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 deer-flow | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Orchestrating complex, long-running AI agent workflows for research, coding, and content creation.
✨ Key Features: • Super Agent Harness • Sub-agents orchestration • Memory management • Sandbox environments • Extensible skills and tools
📖 Summary: DeerFlow is an open-source super agent harness designed for long-horizon tasks. It orchestrates sub-agents, memory, and sandboxes, powered by extensible skills, to research, code, and create complex outputs over extended periods.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
